entrance (P9721) == * 金峰神社の鳥居 吉野町吉野山 Shrine gate of Kimpu-jinja 2011.4.26 - panoramio.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino}} [[CS:Svatyně Kinpu (Jošino)]] [[EN:Kinpu Shrine]] [[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 金峯神社 |画像 = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|金峯神社 拝殿]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|吉野郡|Q1154558}}{{jalink|吉野町|Q1203731}}{{jalink|吉野山|Q1036938}}1651 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 33.3 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 52 | 経度秒 = 54.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = {{jalink|金山毘古命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大}}）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |地図= Japan Nara }} '''金峯神社'''（きんぷじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|吉野郡|Q1154558}}{{jalink|吉野町|Q1203731}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。{{jalink|吉野山|Q1036938}}最奥の{{jalink|青根ヶ峰|Q11661968}}のそばにあり、吉野山の{{jalink|地主神|Q4160872}}を祭る。 {{jalink|#史跡||境内}}は{{jalink|国際連合教育科学文化機関|Q7809|ユネスコ}}の{{jalink|世界遺産|Q9259}}『{{jalink|紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道|Q611345}}』（{{jalink|2004年|Q2014}}〈{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}16年〉7月登録）の構成資産の一部{{Cite book ja-jp|author=世界遺産登録推進三県協議会|year=2005|title=世界遺産 紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道|publisher=世界遺産登録推進三県協議会（和歌山県・奈良県・三重県）}}、pp.39,75。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山毘古命}}（かなやまひこのみこと）。吉野山の地主神である。 === 歴史 === 創建の経緯などは不明である。中世以降は{{jalink|修験道|Q1483875}}の修行場[https://www.town.yoshino.nara.jp/kanko-event/meisho-kanko/shisetsu/yoshinoyama/kinpu-jinja.html 吉野町ホームページ　金峯神社] であり、『{{jalink|栄花物語|Q1302985}}』には{{jalink|藤原道長|Q433133}}が詣でたことが記されている。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}時代以前の{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}時代には「'''金精明神'''（こんしょうみょうじん）」と呼ばれ、{{jalink|本地仏}}は{{jalink|阿閦如来|Q756612}}、{{jalink|釈迦如来|Q11644537}}、{{jalink|大日如来|Q239847}}（{{jalink|金剛界}}）とされていた。金精の名は金峯山は黄金を蔵する山という信仰があったことが背景にあると思われる『神仏習合の本』 学習研究社 2008年。 === 境内 === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|義経隠れ塔]] * 本殿 * 拝殿 - {{jalink|吉野神宮|Q11414497}}の旧拝殿を移築したもの[https://yamatoji.nara-kankou.or.jp/01shaji/01jinja/04south_area/kimpujinja/ 奈良旅ネット＜奈良県観光公式サイト＞　金峯神社]。 * 義経隠れ塔 - {{jalink|大正|Q329174}}時代初年に再建。金峯神社拝殿脇の小径を下った場所に建つ宝形造・檜皮葺きの簡素な塔。{{jalink|源頼朝|Q242800}}に追われた{{jalink|源義経|Q310445}}がここに隠れていたという。その際に追っ手に囲まれてしまい、屋根を蹴破って逃げたといわれ「'''蹴抜の塔'''（けのけのとう）」とも呼ばれる。 === 文化財 === === 国宝 === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|金銅藤原道長経筒（寛弘四年、国宝）]] * 金銅藤原道長経筒 1口 *: {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}27年）11月22日、{{jalink|国宝|Q1139795}}（{{jalink|考古資料|Q2686349}}）に指定昭和28年3月11日文化財保護委員会告示第17号。金峯山経塚出土品のひとつで、表面に刻された銘文中に「寛弘四年八月十一日」の年記がある。 *: 高さ35.8センチメートル、口径15.7センチメートル。{{jalink|藤原道長|Q433133}}が{{jalink|寛弘|Q2736722}}4年（{{jalink|1007年|Q19233}}）に{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}の金峯山経塚（奈良県{{jalink|吉野郡|Q1154558}}{{jalink|天川村|Q1203501}}）に埋納した厚手で大ぶりな円筒形経筒である（経塚とは、この世に{{jalink|弥勒菩薩|Q193461|弥勒仏}}が出現するとされる遠い未来まで経典を保存するために経箱や経筒を埋め、盛土したもの）。鍛銅製で鍍金が施され、器表に24行で510文字の銘文が刻まれており、埋経の趣旨と経緯が記されている。年代の明らかな経塚遺物としては最古のものである。銘文の内容は道長の日記『{{jalink|御堂関白記|Q11488903}}』の記載とも符合し、歴史的にも重要なものである。銘文の執筆者は不明だが、道長自筆する説や石田茂作 矢島恭介 『金峯山経塚の研究』帝室博物館学報八、1938年。、道長と関係が深い能書家・{{jalink|藤原行成|Q3076818}}とする説がある宮川禎一 「藤原道長経筒に刻まれた願文をめぐって ―その文字は藤原行成の筆跡か―」『学叢』第37号、京都国立博物館、pp.9-38。{{jalink|京都国立博物館|Q147286}}考古部門に{{jalink|寄託 (日本法)|Q65262258|寄託}}。中に入っていたとされる紺紙金字経は、下記の他に{{jalink|東京国立博物館|Q653433}}[https://emuseum.nich.go.jp/detail?langId=ja&webView=&content_base_id=100448 長徳四年紺紙金字経 - e国宝]や{{jalink|五島美術館|Q2374140}}が所蔵している経筒の説明は以下の資料による。 * 『週刊朝日百科 日本の国宝10』、朝日新聞社、1997、pp.4-298, 4-299 * 大阪市立美術館編『祈りの道 - 吉野・熊野・高野の名宝 -』（特別展図録）、毎日新聞社、2004、p.290。 === 重要文化財 === * 大和国金峯山経塚出土品 *: 以下8点一括。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（昭和28年）3月31日、{{jalink|重要文化財|Q1188622}}（{{jalink|考古資料|Q2686349}}）に指定{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9744 |title=大和国金峯山経塚出土品 |work=[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] |publisher={{jalink|文化庁|Q1009519}} |accessdate=2010-01-22}}、1955年（昭和30年）2月2日追加指定昭和30年2月2日文化財保護委員会告示第6号。1953年に「鍍銀経箱 附 金銅経箱台残闕、紺紙金字無量義経残闕」として指定。1955年に他の経典残欠類を追加指定のうえ、指定名称を現行のものに改めた。。 ** 鍍銀経箱 ** 金銅経箱台残闕 ** 紺紙金字法華経 巻一、三、四、五、六、七残闕 巻四長□□□（徳四年）奥書 ** 紺紙金字無量義経残闕 長徳四年奥書 ** 紺紙金字弥勒上生経残闕 ** 紺紙金字弥勒下生経残闕 ** 紺紙金字法華経 巻三、四、六、七残闕 ** 紺紙金字無量義経 * 鉄鐔 卒塔婆透し 1箇（附：蒔絵箱1箇） *: {{jalink|1925年|Q18107}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}14年）4月24日、重要文化財（工芸品）に指定{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6299 |title=鉄鐔卒塔婆透シ |work=[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] |publisher={{jalink|文化庁|Q1009519}} |accessdate=2010-01-22}}。 === 史跡 === * 大峯奥駈道 - 金峯神社境内は、国の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}「{{jalink|大峯奥駈道|Q11434795}}」の一部である。{{jalink|2002年|Q1987}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}14年）12月19日指定{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3359 |title=大峯奥駈道 |work=[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index 国指定文化財等データベース] |publisher={{jalink|文化庁|Q1009519}} |accessdate=2010-01-18}}。 === アクセス === {{jalink|近鉄吉野線|Q901085}}{{jalink|吉野駅 (奈良県)|Q8056197|吉野駅}}より{{jalink|吉野ロープウェイ|Q8056195}}乗り換え「吉野山」下車、徒歩約1時間45分。または吉野山駅からバスで23分の「奥千本口」下車、徒歩5分。 === 脚注 === === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 畑中章宏『廃仏毀釈』筑摩書房、2021年 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|金峯山寺|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|大峯山寺|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kinpu Shrine | image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kinpu Shrine | image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kinpu Shrine | image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kinpu Shrine | image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]]

[[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kinpu Shrine | image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yoshino District|Q1154558}}{{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}{{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{jalink|Yoshino|Q1203731}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{jalink|Yoshinoyama|Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{jalink|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{jalink|Shugendo|Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{jalink|Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{jalink|Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{jalink|Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{jalink|Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{jalink|Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{jalink|Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668}} * {{jalink|Omine-san-ji|Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668|WD=Q1741668}} * {{ill|lt=Omine-san-ji|Q3077344|WD=Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668|WD=Q1741668}} * {{ill|lt=Omine-san-ji|Q3077344|WD=Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668|WD=Q1741668}} * {{ill|lt=Omine-san-ji|Q3077344|WD=Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668|WD=Q1741668}} * {{ill|lt=Omine-san-ji|Q3077344|WD=Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[FR:Kinpu-jinja (Yoshino)]] [[IT:Santuario Kinpu]] [[JA:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] [[TR:Kinpu Tapınağı]] [[ZH:金峯神社 (吉野町)]] {{wikidata link|Q3197197}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Kinpu Shrine | image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} 1651 | deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | annual_festival = Third Sunday of October }} '''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama. Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345}}" (registered July 2004).World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama === History === The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133}}. Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai). === Precinct === [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]] * Honden * Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497}} * Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445}} === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === [[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]] * Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1) : Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007." : Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum. === Important Cultural Properties === * Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province : Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper. * Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box) : Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925. === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795}} : The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002). === Access === From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot. === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Akihiro Hatanaka, ''Haibutsu Kishaku'', Chikuma Shobo, 2021. === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kinpusen-ji|Q1741668|WD=Q1741668}} * {{ill|lt=Omine-san-ji|Q3077344|WD=Q3077344}} {{Commonscat}} {{神道 横}} {{紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きんふしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:紀伊山地の霊場と参詣道]] [[Category:吉野・大峯]] [[Category:大峯奥駈道]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名きんふしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:御嶽神社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (考古・歴史資料)]] [[Category:奈良県の国宝]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:吉野町の建築物]] [[Category:吉野町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kinpu Shrine (Q3197197)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{nihongo|'''Kinpu Shrine'''|金峯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
