== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine
| name = Kinpu Shrine
| image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]<br />Haiden<br />{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}
| location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} 1651
| deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}
| status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
| architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
| annual_festival = Third Sunday of October
}}
'''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama.

Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}" (registered July 2004).<ref name="sanken">World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75.</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama

=== History ===
The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985|1=Eiga Monogatari}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}.

Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai).

=== Precinct ===
[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]]
* Honden
* Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497|1=Yoshino Shrine}}
* Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}

=== Cultural properties ===
=== National Treasure ===
[[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]]
* Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1)
: Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007."
: Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum.

=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province
: Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper.
* Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box)
: Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925.

=== Historic site ===
* {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795|1=Ōmine Okugakemichi}}
: The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002).<ref name="Bunka"/>

=== Access ===
From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station|1=Yoshino Station (Nara)}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot.

{{nihongo|'''Kinpu Shrine'''|金峯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine
| name = Kinpu Shrine
| image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]<br />Haiden<br />{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}
| location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|WD=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} 1651
| deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}
| status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
| architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
| annual_festival = Third Sunday of October
}}
'''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|WD=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama.

Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|WD=Q611345|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}" (registered July 2004).<ref name="sanken">World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75.</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama

=== History ===
The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|WD=Q1302985|1=Eiga Monogatari}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|WD=Q433133|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}.

Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai).

=== Precinct ===
[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]]
* Honden
* Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|WD=Q11414497|1=Yoshino Shrine}}
* Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|WD=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}

=== Cultural properties ===
=== National Treasure ===
[[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]]
* Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1)
: Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007."
: Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum.

=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province
: Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper.
* Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box)
: Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925.

=== Historic site ===
* {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|WD=Q11434795|1=Ōmine Okugakemichi}}
: The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002).<ref name="Bunka"/>

=== Access ===
From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|WD=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station|1=Yoshino Station (Nara)}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot.

{{nihongo|'''Kinpu Shrine'''|金峯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine
| name = Kinpu Shrine
| image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]<br />Haiden<br />{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}
| location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|qid=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|qid=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|qid=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} 1651
| deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}
| status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
| architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
| annual_festival = Third Sunday of October
}}
'''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|qid=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|qid=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama.

Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|qid=Q611345|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}" (registered July 2004).<ref name="sanken">World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75.</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama

=== History ===
The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|qid=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|qid=Q1302985|1=Eiga Monogatari}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|qid=Q433133|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}.

Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai).

=== Precinct ===
[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]]
* Honden
* Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|qid=Q11414497|1=Yoshino Shrine}}
* Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|qid=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}

=== Cultural properties ===
=== National Treasure ===
[[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]]
* Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1)
: Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007."
: Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum.

=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province
: Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper.
* Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box)
: Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925.

=== Historic site ===
* {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|qid=Q11434795|1=Ōmine Okugakemichi}}
: The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002).<ref name="Bunka"/>

=== Access ===
From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|qid=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station|1=Yoshino Station (Nara)}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot.

{{nihongo|'''Kinpu Shrine'''|金峯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shrine
| name = Kinpu Shrine
| image = [[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino01.JPG|270px|Kinpu Shrine haiden]]<br />Haiden<br />{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}
| location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino District|Q1154558|qid=Q1154558|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|qid=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|qid=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} 1651
| deity = {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}
| status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Meishin-taisha), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
| architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
| annual_festival = Third Sunday of October
}}
'''Kinpu Shrine''' (きんぷじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|lt=Yoshino|Q1203731|qid=Q1203731|1=Yoshino, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (meishin-taisha) and former gosha. Located near Aonegamine at the innermost area of {{ill|lt=Yoshinoyama|Q1036938|qid=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}}, it enshrines the local tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama.

Its precinct is part of the UNESCO World Heritage property "{{ill|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|Q611345|qid=Q611345|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}" (registered July 2004).<ref name="sanken">World Heritage Registration Promotion Council of Three Prefectures (2005), pp.39, 75.</ref>

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} (main deity), tutelary deity of Yoshinoyama

=== History ===
The foundation is unknown. Since medieval times it has been a site for {{ill|lt=Shugendo|Q1483875|qid=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} practice, and the ''{{ill|lt=Eiga Monogatari|Q1302985|qid=Q1302985|1=Eiga Monogatari}}'' records a visit by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|Q433133|qid=Q433133|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}}.

Before the Meiji period, during shinbutsu-shugo, it was called "Konsho Myojin," with associated honji-butsu identified as Akshobhya, Shaka, and Dainichi (Kongokai).

=== Precinct ===
[[File:Konbu Shrine, Yoshino03.JPG|thumb|right|Yoshitsune-kakure-to]]
* Honden
* Haiden - transferred former haiden of {{ill|lt=Yoshino Jingu|Q11414497|qid=Q11414497|1=Yoshino Shrine}}
* Yoshitsune Hidden Tower (also called Kenuke-no-to) - rebuilt in early Taisho period; linked to legend of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Q310445|qid=Q310445|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}

=== Cultural properties ===
=== National Treasure ===
[[ファイル:Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra case (Kinpu Shrine, Yoshino-gun, Nara Prefecture).jpg|thumb|150px|Gilt-bronze sutra tube of Fujiwara no Michinaga (1007), National Treasure]]
* Gilt-bronze Fujiwara no Michinaga sutra tube (1)
: Designated National Treasure (archaeological material) on November 22, 1952. One of the Kinpusan sutra-mound finds; inscription includes date "August 11, 1007."
: Height 35.8 cm, mouth diameter 15.7 cm. A large thick cylindrical copper tube with gilding, engraved with 510 characters in 24 lines explaining the motive and process of sutra burial. It is one of the oldest securely dated sutra-mound artifacts and aligns with records in Michinaga's diary ''Mido Kanpakuki''. Deposited in the Kyoto National Museum.

=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Excavated objects from Kinpusan sutra mound in Yamato Province
: Group designation (8 categories) as Important Cultural Property in 1953, with additions in 1955, including a silver-plated sutra box, remains of a gilt-bronze stand, and multiple fragments of gold-lettered sutras on indigo paper.
* Iron tsuba with stupa-openwork design (with maki-e box)
: Designated Important Cultural Property (craft) in 1925.

=== Historic site ===
* {{ill|lt=Omine Okugakemichi|Q11434795|qid=Q11434795|1=Ōmine Okugakemichi}}
: The shrine precinct is part of this nationally designated historic site (designated December 19, 2002).<ref name="Bunka"/>

=== Access ===
From Kintetsu Yoshino Line {{ill|lt=Yoshino Station (Nara)|Q8056197|qid=Q8056197|lt=Yoshino Station|1=Yoshino Station (Nara)}}, transfer to the Yoshino Ropeway to "Yoshinoyama," then about 1 hour 45 minutes on foot; or bus from Yoshinoyama Station to "Okusenbon-guchi" (about 23 minutes), then 5 minutes on foot.
